notes:
I played this game a few years back on Proton Experiemental and an older Nvidia driver with no issues. Reinstalling it and trying to play on the latest Debian Testing has the game close immediately on start before the splash screen appears.

I tried all the older documented tricks for getting this working: bypassing EAC via changing the executable filename, using different versions of Proton (Experimental, Bleeding Edge, Stable 9, Stable 8, etc),  passing various launch options. The only thing I haven't tried is downgrading my system packages which may fix the issue, but I am not willing to try.

PROTON_LOG=1 gives a pretty consistent error message "Failed to create Vulkan device, vr -3." and then crashing with an EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION.
title: ELDEN RING

notes:
I played through the entire base game and then some (350+ hours) on this same PC. Now update 1.12 won't start and crashes at the launcher (I blame Easy Anti-Cheat). I tried Proton 9.0-2, 8.0-5, 7.0-6, Proton Experimental, as well as GE-Proton9-7. Options gamemoderun %command% and PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command% make no difference.

The game thankfully still runs on my Steam Deck OLED with default launch options.
